3#include "archetype.hpp"

15namespace world {
16 // Separated per cacheline, chunked arena bump allocator.
17 constexpr size_t CHUNK_SIZE = 64;
18 constexpr size_t LOG2_CHUNK_SIZE = 6;
19 constexpr size_t THREAD_COUNT = 16;
20
21 inline size_t alignup(size_t x, size_t align) {
22 return (x + align - 1) & ~(align - 1);
23 }
24 inline size_t aligndown(size_t x, size_t align) { return x & ~(align - 1); }
25
26 template <typename T>
27 class alignas(64) Allocator {
28 public:
29 Allocator() {};
30 ~Allocator() { // TODO! properly ensure memory is cleared.
31 for (auto* c : m_chunks) {
32 //delete c;
33 }
34 };
35
36 struct Chunk {
37 size_t m_count = 0; // Amount of elements in chunk, full if == chunkCapacity.
38 T m_data[CHUNK_SIZE];
39 };
40
41 T& push(T&& t) {
42 if (m_lastChunk->m_count == CHUNK_SIZE) {
43 m_count ++;
44 m_chunks.emplace_back(new Chunk);
45 m_lastChunk = m_chunks.back();
46 }
47
48 m_lastChunk->m_data[m_lastChunk->m_count] = std::forward<T>(t);
49 return m_lastChunk->m_data[m_lastChunk->m_count++];
50 }
51
52 size_t count() {return m_count;}
53
54 // AHHHHHHH
55 T& get(size_t index) {
56 size_t chunk = index >> LOG2_CHUNK_SIZE;
57 size_t element = index & (CHUNK_SIZE - 1);
58
59 Chunk* c = m_chunks[chunk];
60 return c->m_data[element];
61 }
62
63 private:
64 std::vector<Chunk*> m_chunks {new Chunk}; // Pointer table
65
66 size_t m_count = 0;
67 Chunk* m_lastChunk = m_chunks.back(); // Fast append path.
68 Chunk* m_base = m_lastChunk;
69 };
70
71
72 // Collection of PageBlocks belinging to a isngle Archetype (per thread).
73 struct ArchetypePool {
74 Allocator<platform::PageBlock> m_blocks;
75 size_t m_waterline = 0;
76
77 size_t m_blockCap = 0;
78 size_t m_bitsetSize = 0;
79 size_t m_count = 0;
80
81 size_t m_archetypeIndex;
82 std::vector<size_t> m_offsets;
83
84 template <typename Arch>
85 static ArchetypePool createPool(size_t poolIndex) {
86 ArchetypePool pool;
87
88 // Find largest element for alignment
89 size_t lComp = Arch::canonical_types::ids[0].size;
90 for (auto &id : Arch::canonical_types::ids) {
91 if (id.size > lComp)
92 lComp = id.size;
93 }
94
95 size_t reqESize = (Arch::size << 3) + 2; // Required size per entity in bits. (1 additional for activity bitset).
96 size_t nArrays = Arch::length + 2;
97 size_t bitPadding = (63 * nArrays * 8) + 7;
98 size_t lbCap = ((platform::PAGEBLOCK_SIZE << 3) - bitPadding) / reqESize; // Lower bound capacity assuming 64 byte alignment per entity.
99
100 pool.m_blockCap = lbCap;
101 pool.m_bitsetSize = alignup(pool.m_blockCap / 8, 64); // Capacity / 8 for bitset size, aligned to a cache boundary.
102
103 size_t alignedOffsets = 0;
104 size_t index = 0;
105
106 std::vector<size_t> canonicalOffsets;
107 canonicalOffsets.reserve(Arch::length);
108
109 for (auto &id : Arch::canonical_types::ids) {
110 canonicalOffsets.emplace_back(alignedOffsets);
111 alignedOffsets += alignup(id.size * lbCap, 64);
112 index++;
113 }
114
115 pool.m_offsets = std::move(canonicalOffsets);
116
117 // ensure atleast m_waterline is valid.
118 pool.insertBlock();
119 return pool;
120 }
121
122 template <size_t Thread, typename ...Ts>
123 inline uint64_t insert(Ts&&... components) {
124 using Canonical = Archetype<Ts...>::canonical_types;
125 scan:
126 platform::PageBlock& block = m_blocks.get(m_waterline);
127
128 if (block.m_count < m_blockCap) {
129 for (;;) {
130 uint64_t* __restrict active = reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(block.m_base) + block.m_waterline;
131 while (*active == UINT64_MAX) {active++;}
132
133 uint64_t word = *active;
134 uint64_t inv = ~word;
135 uint64_t bit = inv & -inv; // isolate lowest zero bit
136 *active = word | bit;
137
138 int index = __builtin_ctzll(bit);
139 block.m_waterline = active - reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(block.m_base);
140
141 ECS_ASSERT(m_waterline < (1ULL<<24) && index < UINT32_MAX, "Exceeds entity index bounds: ({}, {})", m_waterline, index);
142 auto values = std::tuple<std::decay_t<Ts>...>(std::forward<Ts>(components)...);
143
144 world::meta::for_each_type_indexed<Canonical>::apply([&]<typename T, size_t I>() {
145 auto &value = std::get<T>(values);
146 *(T*)(block.m_base + (m_bitsetSize * 2) + m_offsets[I] + (index * sizeof(T))) = value;
147 });
148
149 // Entitites in the block
150 block.m_count++;
151
152 // [THREAD(8)][Archetype(16)][Block(16)][Index(24)]
153 return (Thread << 56) | (m_archetypeIndex << 40) | (m_waterline << 24) | index; // Combine
154 }
155 } else {
156 // Insert a new block if waterline exceeds count.
157 if (++m_waterline >= m_count) insertBlock();
158 goto scan;
159 }
160 }
161
162 // Does NOT take thread nor archetype into account, will just pop this id from the current pool if called.
163 inline void removeEntity(uint64_t entity) {
164 size_t blockIndex = (entity >> 24) & ((1ULL << 16) - 1);
165 size_t index = entity & ((1ULL << 24) - 1);
166 size_t wordIndex = index >> 6;
167
168 platform::PageBlock& block = m_blocks.get(blockIndex);
169 uint64_t* active = reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(block.m_base) + wordIndex;
170 *active &= ~(1Ull << (index & 63));
171
172 block.m_waterline = std::min(block.m_waterline, wordIndex);
173 m_waterline = std::min(m_waterline, blockIndex);
174 }
175
176 inline void insertBlock() {
177 platform::PageBlock block;
178 std::memset(block.m_base, 0, m_bitsetSize * 2);
179 m_blocks.push(std::move(block));
180 m_count++;
181
182 //std::cout << "Inserted new block: " << m_count << std::endl;
183 }
184 };
185
186 class StorageArena {
187 public:
188 StorageArena() {};
189 ~StorageArena() {};
190
191 template <typename Arch, size_t Thread>
192 ArchetypePool& ensure_at() {
193 static ArchetypePool& pool = m_pools[Thread].push(ArchetypePool::createPool<Arch>(m_pools[Thread].count()));
194 m_signatures[Thread].push_back(Arch::signature);
195 return pool;
196 }
197
198 // Retrieve from a generated index
199 ArchetypePool& get(size_t thread, size_t index) {
200 ECS_ASSERT(thread < THREAD_COUNT && index < m_pools[thread].count(), "Index out of bounds.");
201 return m_pools[thread].get(index);
202 }
203
204 private:
205 std::array<Allocator<ArchetypePool>, THREAD_COUNT> m_pools;
206 // Parallel structure to the underlying pools, to avoid fragmentation whhen scanning.
207 std::array<std::vector<Signature>, THREAD_COUNT> m_signatures;
208 };
209}
